message:
fix(learn): exclude node prop from ReactMarkdown heading components

ReactMarkdown passes a `node` AST prop to custom components. When spread
to DOM elements, it renders as `node="[object Object]"` which breaks
Tailwind prose CSS selectors, causing headings to render unstyled.

Fix: Destructure `node` separately so it's not spread to DOM elements.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -404,12 +404,18 @@ export function LessonContent({ lesson, content }: Props) { rehypePlugins={[rehypeHighlight]} components={{ // Demote all headings by 1 level since lesson.title is the page h1 - h1: ({ children, ...props }) =>

{children}

, - h2: ({ children, ...props }) =>

{children}

, - h3: ({ children, ...props }) =>

{children}

, - h4: ({ children, ...props }) =>
{children}
, - h5: ({ children, ...props }) =>
{children}
, - h6: ({ children, ...props }) =>
{children}
, + // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars + h1: ({ children, node, ...props }) =>

{children}

, + // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars + h2: ({ children, node, ...props }) =>

{children}

, + // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars + h3: ({ children, node, ...props }) =>

{children}

, + // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars + h4: ({ children, node, ...props }) =>
{children}
, + // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars + h5: ({ children, node, ...props }) =>
{children}
, + // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars + h6: ({ children, node, ...props }) =>
{children}
, }} > {content}
